title: The Statue That Receives the God — The Hermetic Asclepius and the Animation of Images
author: Hermetic Asclepius

body.text: The Asclepius speaks of statues that receive divine presence. Art, ritual, matter, and celestial power converge so intensely that an image may become a body for a god. The image is a place where presence arrives.

Our ancestors, having discovered the art of making gods, mixed a certain power drawn from the nature of matter into the substance of the statues; and since they could not make souls, they invoked the souls of demons or angels and implanted them in the images by holy and divine rites.

The ancestors build a receptacle. The statue becomes a prepared body — matter shaped and consecrated, like a room swept clean before a guest arrives. The visible is rendered capable of receiving the invisible. The artist and the priest are close kin: both prepare bodies for presence.

body.caption: The image as receptacle — matter shaped to receive what exceeds it.

body.text: These statues, then, knowing and foreknowing all things, predict by lots, by prophets, by dreams and many other means; they send diseases and cure them, they give sorrow and joy according to our merits.

The statue becomes oracle, healer, and judge — all within a single knot of presence. Stone participates in a larger symbolic body: form, rite, name, and celestial timing joined together. A thing becomes alive within relation.
Egypt is an image of heaven; or, to speak more exactly, in Egypt all the operations of the powers which rule and work in heaven have been transferred to earth below.

Egypt is a terrestrial heaven. Its temples and images bring celestial operations into earthly form. Theurgy here is architectural. The temple is a body for the cosmos. The environment teaches consciousness what kind of world it inhabits.

body.caption: Terrestrial heaven — the temple as a body for the cosmos.

body.text: And if it is permitted to speak more boldly, our land is the temple of the whole world.
Ask what kind of body, room, and attention could receive the reality you seek. Theurgy begins by constructing receptacles. The soul itself must become such a statue.
